title: The One With the Race Car Bed

lines.text: Central Perk, the whole gang is there, Ross is telling a story about what happened at work and the rest of the gang are thinking to themselves, denoted by italics.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So I told Carl, 'Nobody, no matter how famous their parents are, nobody is allowed to climb on the dinosaur.' But of course this went in one ear and out.....

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I love how he cares so much about stuff. If I squint I can pretend he's Alan Alda.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh good, another dinosaur story. When are those gonna become extinct?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: If I was a superhero who could fly and be invisible, that would be the best.

lines.speaker: Gunther
lines.text: What does Rachel see in this guy? I love Rachel. I wish she was my wife.
Joey is singing in his head.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Who's singing?
Opening Credits
Monica and Rachel's, the whole gang is there including Janice, they're watching Happy Days.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey. When you guys were kids and you played Happy Days, who were you? I was always Richie.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I was always Joanne.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Question. Was ah, 'Egg the Gellers!' the war cry of your neighbourhood?
A commercial for the Mattress King, Janice's ex-husband, comes on TV.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ewww! Oh! It's the Mattress King!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Booo!!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (to Janice) Don't look honey. Change the channel! Change the channel!

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Wait! Wait! I wanna see this. After I divorce him, half of that kingdom is gonna be mine.

lines.speaker: Matress King
lines.text: (on TV) 'Despair fills the mattress showroom. My kingdom is suddenly without a queen. I'm so depressed I'm going to slash... my prices!! Check it out! Four ninety-nine for a pillow top queen set! I'm going medieval on prices!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What a wank!

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Oh, I cannot believe he's using our divorce to sell mattresses.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I know! And four ninety-nine for a pillow top queen set, who cares about the divorce, those babies will sell themselves. (they all stare at her) And I'm appalled for you by the way.

lines.speaker: Matress King
lines.text: (on TV) I'm close. I'm cheap. I'm the king.
Central Perk, Rachel is on the phone, everyone else is there except Joey.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: 'Okay. (listens) Okay, daddy we'll see you tomorrow night. (listens) Okay bye-bye.' (hangs up)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: We?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Are ah, having dinner with my Dad tomorrow night, I hope that's okay.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh shoot, tomorrow's not so good, I'm supposed to um, fall off the Empire State building and land on a bicycle with no seat. Sorry.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ross, my father doesn't hate you.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Please, he refers to me as 'wethead'.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: But honey he calls everybody by a nickname! Okay, look, I know, all right, just one dinner, please, just one night for me, please. I just want him to love you like I do. (Ross looks at her) All right, well not exactly like I do, but, but, if you do come to dinner, I'll love you like I do in that black thing that you like.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (leaning in) I'll go.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Fine.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Thank you.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hi Gunther.

lines.speaker: Gunther
lines.text: Yeah, we'll see!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (entering) Hey, you guys!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Guess what?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I got a gig!

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Yay!!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: See, that's why I could never be an actor. Because I can't say gig.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, I can't say croissant. (realises) Oh my God!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What's the part?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, it's not a part, no. I'm teaching acting for soap operas down at the Learning Extension.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Come on! That's great.

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Wow!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, yeah. It's like my chance to give something back to the acting community.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Y'know your probably not allowed to sleep with any of your students.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (glares at him) I know!
Mattress King, Monica and Phoebe are shopping for a new mattress.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ugh! I don't know Monica. It feels funny just being here. I mean if you buy a bed from Janice's ex-husband, that's like betraying Chandler.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Not at these prices.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (sees a little kid playing with a race car bed) (to kid) Hi. Y'know in England this car would be on the other side of the store. (the kid just stares at her, and she makes the 'that went right over your head' motion) Woo!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (lying down on a mattress) Oh! Ohhhhh! Oh! Phoebe, come here. Aw, this is my new bed. You gotta feel this bad boy.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Eh, Monica it, it feels so weird, y'know, Chandler's your friend... (hops onto the bed) Oh! Oh my God! Aw, all right take this bed, you can make other friends.

Classroom. Joey is writing his name on the board, but turns around before he's done which causes him to write his name with a downward curve, and he then underlines it, and draws the line right through his name.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Good evening. I'm Mr. Tribbiani. And I will be teaching acting for soap operas. Now um, on my first day as (proudly) Dr. Drake Remoray on Days of Our Lives, (looks for a reaction from his students, and gets none.) I learned that one of the most important things in soap opera acting is reacting, this does not mean acting again, it means, you don't have a line, but someone else just did. And it goes like this. (looks all intense for a moment and then gasps, the students cheer him) Thanks, thanks, a lot. Oh, by the way, before I forget to work in soap operas some of you will have to become much more attractive. All right, moving right along.
Monica and Rachel's, Joey is entering, Phoebe is already there waiting for the delievery guy.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey! Ooh! How was teaching last night?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh it was great. Yeah, you get to say stuff like, 'Hey, the bell doesn't dismiss you, I dismiss you.'

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ooooh, nice.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, and guess what, I got an audition for All My Children.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, yay!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, it's this great part, this boxer named Nick. And I'm so, so right for it, y'know, he's just like me. Except he's a boxer, and has an evil twin.
There is a knock on the door.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh. (goes and answers the door and there is this huge black delievery guy.)

lines.speaker: Guy
lines.text: Dom da-da dom! Here ye! Here ye! Delivery from the Mattress King. (to Phoebe) You Miss Geller?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Guy
lines.text: Sign here. (hands her a clipboard)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, do I have a middle name. All right Monica Velula Geller. It's that bedroom there. (points to Monica's room)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, Monica bought a bed from the Mattress King?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, so please, please, please, don't say anything to Chandler.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You want me to lie to Chandler?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Is that a problem?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, hey, hey Nick the boxer let's see what you got. All right ya, put 'em up. Come on. (they start shadow boxing)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, you're ah, pretty good at this.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, well I had to learn, I was staying at the Y and some off the young men weren't acting Christian enough.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ahh!
Joey throws a punch and just lightly taps her on the shoulder, Phoebe counters with a jab to the nose.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey now!
Phoebe throws another jab, and lands it on Joey's nose, causing it to bleed.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey!!! Oww!! And I'm bleeding.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh! Oh! Oh!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay, great.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Wow! And I'm a vegetarian! All right, all right, well I'm sorry, we'll put some ice on it.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: 'Kay, put your head back.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right. I can't see.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: All right, I have ya. Oh God.

lines.speaker: Guy
lines.text: Which bedroom do ya want it in Miss Geller?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, it's the compulsively neat one by the window, okay.

lines.speaker: Guy
lines.text: Gotcha. (he and his helper walk in carrying the racecar bed.)
Restaurant, Rachel and Ross and Dr. Green are having dinner.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi Daddy!

lines.speaker: Dr. Green
lines.text: This where they put it? What, there no table available in the kitchen! Hello, baby.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You remember Ross.

lines.speaker: Dr. Green
lines.text: Um-hmm.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Nice to see you again Dr. Green.

lines.speaker: Dr. Green
lines.text: So! (they both try to sit next to Rachel but Dr. Green is successful.) (to Ross) How's the library?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ugh, museum.

lines.speaker: Dr. Green
lines.text: What happened to the library?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: There never was a library. I mean there are libraries, its just that I ah, I never worked at one.

lines.speaker: Dr. Green
lines.text: You know what's really good here, the lobster. What do you say shall I just order three.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, if you're really hungry. (Dr. Green stares at him) It was a joke, I made a joke.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, actually Daddy Ross is allergic to lobster.

lines.speaker: Dr. Green
lines.text: What kind of person is allergic to lobster? I guess the kind of person that works at a library.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: It's not a library...

lines.speaker: Dr. Green
lines.text: (interrupting him) I know!! It's a museum! What, you're the only one around here who can make a joke! At least mine was funny. Ah, waiter, we will have two lobsters and a menu. (nods at Ross, and mouths I don't know to the waiter.)
After dinner.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So, Dr. Green, how's the old boat.

lines.speaker: Dr. Green
lines.text: They found rust. You know what rust does to a boat?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: It gives it a nice antiquey look.

lines.speaker: Dr. Green
lines.text: (he stares at Ross) Rust, is boat cancer, Ross.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Wow. I'm sorry, when I was a kid I lost a bike to that. (Rachel giggles at that)

lines.speaker: Dr. Green
lines.text: Excuse me for a moment, will you please, I want to say good night to the Levines, before we go.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay! (picks up a knife and pretends to stab his heart.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Aw honey stop! It's not that bad.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah. (sees the bill) Op! Uh-oh! I think your Dad must've added wrong. He only tipped like four percent.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah. That's Daddy.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That's Daddy?! But doesn't it bother you? You're a waitress.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yes, it bothers me Ross, but y'know if he was a regular at the coffee house, I'd be serving him sneezers.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: So. Ross, I've bugged him about this a million times, he's not gonna change.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You really serve people sneezers?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well um, I don't.

lines.speaker: Dr Green
lines.text: You kids ready?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Thanks again, Dr. Green.

lines.speaker: Dr. Green
lines.text: All right.
Ross takes a twenty and slips it underneath the bill when Dr. Green isn't looking.

lines.speaker: Dr. Green
lines.text: Oh, wait, wait, wait, wait, I think I forgot my receipt.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, ah, you don't need that.

lines.speaker: Dr. Green
lines.text: Why not?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: The carbon, it's messy, I mean it gets on your fingers and causes, the, the ah, night blindness.

lines.speaker: Dr. Green
lines.text: (gets his receipt and notices the twenty) What is this? Who put a twenty down here? Huh?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, yeah, that would be me, um, I have, I have a problem I-I tip way too much, way, way, too much, it's a sickness really.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah it is, it is. (to Ross) We really, really have to do something about that.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I know.

lines.speaker: Dr. Green
lines.text: Excuse me, you think I'm cheap?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh Daddy, no he didn't mean anything by that, he really didn't.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Nothing I do means anything, really.

lines.speaker: Dr. Green
lines.text: This is nice. I pay two hundred dollars for dinner, you put down twenty, and you come out looking like Mr. Big Shot. You really want to be Mr. Big Shot? Here, I'll tell you what, you pay the whole bill, Mr. Big Shot, all right. (rips up the bill, and throws it at Ross, then leaves)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well Mr. Big Shot is better than 'wethead'.
Classroom, Joey is lecturing on facial expressions.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay, some tricks of the trade. Now, I've never been able to cry as an actor, so if I'm in a scene where I have to cry, I cut a hole in my pocket, take a pair of tweezers, and just start pulling. Or ah, or, let's say I wanna convey that I've just done something evil. That would be the basic 'I have a fishhook in my eyebrow and I like it' (Does it by raising one eyebrow, and showing off the pretend fishhook.) Okay, let's say I've just gotten bad news, well all I do there is try and divide 232 by 13. (looks all confused) And that's how it's done. Great soap opera acting tonight everybody, class dismissed.

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: Hey, Mr. Trib.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey-hey.

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: Guess what, I got an audition!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Awww, one of my students got an audition. I'm so proud.

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: I was wondering if you would consider coaching me for it?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You bet! What's the part?

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: Oh it's great, it's a role on All My Children, Nick the boxer.
Joey does the '232 divided by 13 bad news' look.
Commercial Break
Hallway, Ross and Rachel are returning from dinner.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You had to do it, didn't you? You couldn't just leave it alone.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Four percent. Okay. I tip more than that when there's a bug in my food.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ross, tonight was about the two of you getting along. (Ross groans and rubs his neck) Oh, would you just see my chiropractor, already.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, I'm gonna go to a doctor who went to school in a mini-mall.
they go into Monica and Rachel's, and see Phoebe hopping around.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey Pheebs, what are you doing?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I'm, I'm freaking out! Monica kinda trusted me with something and she shouldn't have! All right, I haven't lived here in a while, so I have to ask you something. Does Monica still turn on the lights in her bedroom?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Um. yeah.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I am soo dead. (goes to Monica's room)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: All right, look, here's the bottom line Ross, this is fixable, if we act fast, okay. So, I'll invite him to brunch tomorrow and you can make nice.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Look, honey, I have tried to make nice, it doesn't work.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, look, Ross, I realise that my Father is difficult, but that's why you have got to be the bigger man here.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Look sweetie, I could be the bigger man, I could be the biggest man, I could be a big, huge, giant man, and it still wouldn't make any difference, except that I could pick your Father up and say 'Like me! Like me tiny doctor!'

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, well can't you just try it one more time Ross? For me? For me?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Rachel one brunch is not gonna solve anything. You gotta face it, okay we're never gonna get along.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, well you are just gonna have too, okay. Because I already got a Mother and a Father who cannot stay in the same room together, okay, I don't wanna have to have a separate room for you too!! (starts to cry)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, okay, okay. (hugs her) I'll get the bagels.
Monica's bedroom, Phoebe is trying to hide the bed from Monica.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (sees the bed) What's this?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Isn't it cool! Varoom! Varoom!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: This is not the bed I ordered!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I know, you must've won like a contest or something!
Phoebe starts to make a sound like a car accelerating

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Phoebe!
Phoebe makes a sound like a car screeching to a halt.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Why is this car in my bedroom?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I'm sorry, okay, I-I wasn't looking, and the store says that they won't take it back because you signed for it...

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: When did I sign for it?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: When I was you! Y'know what, it's all Joey's fault, 'cause he left his nose open!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Did you make brownies today?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Knock, knock.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (to Phoebe) Quick, take off your dress, he won't notice the bed.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey, I'm going for sushi does anybody want.. (enters and sees the bed) Whoa-whoa, somebody missed the off ramp.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: It's Monica's bed. What?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay. (to Monica) It's a racecar.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: So. This has always been Monica's bed, what you're just noticing now, how self-involved are you?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, well it this bed isn't new, how come there is plastic on the mattress?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Sometimes I have bad dreams. (starts to break down, and Phoebe offers her, her hand to comfort her.)
Classroom, Joey is coaching his student.

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: Look, I just saw my best friends brains smeared across the canvas, that's not gonna be me, not me.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Wow! That was good. That was...(points to his pocket) Tweezers?

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Whoa. That was really good.

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: Thanks, any suggestions?
Joey gets the evil look on his face.
Central Perk, Chandler, Monica, and Phoebe are there, yelling at Joey.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You told him to play the boxer gay!!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, I-I might've said supergay.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You totally screwed him over.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Joey, you're this guy's teacher. I mean how could you do this?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Because, Monica, the guy's so good, and I really, really want this part.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, if you really, really want it, then it's okay.
Monica and Rachel's, Rachel is greeting her Father for their brunch.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (opening the door) Hi Daddy.

lines.speaker: Dr. Green
lines.text: Baby. Ross.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Dr. Green. How are you? (offers his hand, and Dr. Green puts his scarf on it.)

lines.speaker: Dr. Green
lines.text: Thanks for dinner last night.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Thank you for teaching me a valuable lesson.

lines.speaker: Dr. Green
lines.text: Nice hair. What'd ya do? Swim here?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (to Rachel) Okay, that's it, I can't take it anymore.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What? What? He's interested in you. He-he likes your hair, he just wants to know how you got here.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, please. Sweetie it's hopeless, okay, I'm just gonna go. (starts to leave rubbing his neck)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Look, look I'm sorry. It's just that....

lines.speaker: Dr. Green
lines.text: Ross? What's with the neck?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: He's got this thing. And I keep telling him to go to my chiropractor...

lines.speaker: Dr. Green
lines.text: You're still going to that chiropractor, that man couldn't get into medical school in Extapa!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Thank you! That's what I keep saying.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Excuse me, Dr. Bobby happens to be an excellent doctor.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Uh.

lines.speaker: Dr. Green
lines.text: Wait a minute, his name is Dr. Bobby?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well that's his last name.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: And his first name.

lines.speaker: Dr. Green
lines.text: He's Bobby Bobby?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: It's Robert Bobby.

lines.speaker: Dr. Green
lines.text: Oh.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: And um, excuse me, he helps me.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh-ho please. Ask her how?

lines.speaker: Dr. Green
lines.text: What do you need help for?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: With my alignment. I've got one leg shorter than the other.

lines.speaker: Dr. Green
lines.text: Oh God!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Argue with that.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What? It's true, my right leg is two inches shorter.

lines.speaker: Dr. Green
lines.text: Come on! You're just titling! (to Ross) Her legs are fine!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I know that!

lines.speaker: Dr. Green
lines.text: So, why do you let her go to a chiropractor for?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I'm sorry, let her?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What can I do, she doesn't listen to me about renter's insurance either.

lines.speaker: Dr. Green
lines.text: Wait a minute, you don't have renter's insurance?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Dr. Green
lines.text: Well what if somebody steals something? How are you gonna run after him with one leg shorter than the other?!
Both he and Ross start laughing

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey, would you ah, would you like some juice?

lines.speaker: Dr. Green
lines.text: I'd love some juice. Thanks.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay. (to Rachel) Wow! This is going so well. Did you see us? Did you see?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah honey, I'm standing right there! Why didn't you just tell him about the mole I haven't got checked yet.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Excellent!
Classroom, Joey is talking to his students.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (sadly) There will come a time in each of your careers when you'll have a chance to screw over another soap opera actor. I had such an opportunity in the recent, present. And I'm ashamed to say that I took it, I advised a fellow actor to play a role, homosexually. Yeah, we both auditioned for the part, and uh, as it turned out, they ah, they liked the stupid gay thing and cast him. And now, he's got a two year contract opposite Susan Luchhi, the first lady of daytime television, and me, me I'm stuck here teaching a bunch of people, most of whom are too ugly to even be on TV. I'm sorry, I'm sorry, I'm sorry. (he gets a huge round of applause from his students.) Thank you.
Mattress King, Monica is trying to return her bed.

lines.speaker: Jester
lines.text: Uh, may I help you?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah, I talked to you on the phone, I'm the lady that got stuck with the racecar bed.

lines.speaker: Jester
lines.text: Look, it's like I told you, there's nothing I can do. You signed for it, Monica Velula Geller.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right, Jester man, look we wanna see the king.

lines.speaker: Jester
lines.text: Nobody sees the king!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh-ho-kay, I'm talking to the king. (starts to go to a back room)

lines.speaker: Jester
lines.text: Hey! You can't go back there!
Joey goes to the door, but stops and looks through the window at Janice and the Mattress King, her ex-husband, kissing.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Oh my God.
Joey fakes a scream.
Closing Credits
Monica's bedroom, Chandler is playing with the bed.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Varrrrrroom! Hey! Watch it lady! Varrrrrrrrrrom! (makes a screeching sound as he pretends to stomp on the brakes.) Hey-hey good lookin'! (honks the bed's little horn on the steering wheel.) Varrrrrrrrroom. (notices Rachel and stops) All right, I'll leave. My bed's so boring.
End
